channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump signs order to expand White House control of independent agencies
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aiming to expand the White House’s authority over various independent regulatory agencies, a move likely to prompt legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: A comprehensive look at DOGE's firings and layoffs so far
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of federal government employees have been shown the door in the first month of President Donald Trump's administration as the White House and its Department of Government Efficiency fire both new and career workers, tell agency leaders to plan for “large-scale reductions
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Really, who were those masked men? New series of books probes backstories of Marvel superheroes
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If you love movies or comics, you've likely followed the adventures of Spider-Man,Iron Man and other superheroes. A new line of books will let you explore the back stories of their creation.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Latest: Trump signs executive order to study how to expand IVF
item.description: Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order meant to expand access to and reduce costs of in vitro fertilization.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Carolina high school football players killed in ATV crash after house party; 3 adults charged
item.description: Three North Carolina adults have been arrested and charged in connection with a fatal August ATV crash that left two Rosman High School students dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dramatic dashcam footage shows moment Sacramento driver flies off busy overpass: video
item.description: Dashcam footage captured the dramatic moment when a vehicle flew off the Interstate 5 overpass and dropped on to a busy Sacramento freeway on Presidents' Day, injuring only the driver.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Illinois governor to back 'screen-free schools' and join national trend to ban cellphones in class
item.description: Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker is aiming to improve student achievement, social interaction and the mental health of public school students by proposing a statewide ban on cellphones in classrooms, an idea that is rapidly gaining traction nationally regardless of political persuasion.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: USDA working to rescind terminations for fired officials working on bird flu efforts
item.description: The accidental firings come amid the Trump administration’s sweeping push to reduce the size of the government and cut the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KREX 5

item.title: Native American activist released from prison will be welcomed to North Dakota home
item.description: BELCOURT, N.D. (AP) — Supporters of Native American activist Leonard Peltier plan to welcome him back to his North Dakota community on Wednesday, a day after his release from a Florida prison where he had been serving a life sentence in the 1975 killings of two FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Winter's next wave of storms takes aim at the East Coast
item.description: The latest in a repeated pattern of winter storms is taking direct aim at the East Coast where heavy snow and some ice is forecast in several states.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley And John Raby, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Justice Department and New York Mayor Adams face judge's scrutiny in bid to dismiss criminal charges
item.description: Justice Department lawyers and New York City Mayor Eric Adams are set to face a federal judge who's signaling that he's unlikely to rubber stamp their request to drop corruption charges weeks before an April trial.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Brutal polar vortex cold shutters Midwest schools, breaks North Dakota record
item.description: More than 95 million people are facing gripping cold Tuesday as a polar vortex sends temperatures plunging to record levels, closing schools, bursting pipes and forcing communities to set up more temporary shelters for the homeless.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Father-son duo survives night in Utah wilderness after finding 'miracle' backpack
item.description: In January, a 15-year-old boy was rescued from a southern Utah trail, but left his backpack behind. Weeks later, a father and son found that backpack and used it to survive.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: BBB warns of scammers offering stimulus checks while impersonating IRS
item.description: Scammers posing as the IRS have told people they are eligible for a $1,400 stimulus check and asked for recipients' personal information.
item.dc:creator: Leah Crowley, NewsNation

item.title: Celebration of life announced for Barrington skater, mother killed in DC plane crash
item.description: The mother and son were returning from a U.S. Figure Skating development camp in Wichita, Kansas when their flight collided with an Army helicopter just before landing.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Turley, NewsNation

item.title: Questions emerge about what may have caused Delta plane to burst into flames and flip over
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Investigators will consider the weather conditions, as well as the possibility of human error or an aircraft malfunction as they try to determine why a Delta Air Lines jet burst into flames and flipped upside down as it tried to land in Toronto, aviation experts said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Watch: Trump signs executive order at Mar-a-Lago expanding IVF access
item.description: "The Order directs policy recommendations to protect IVF access and aggressively reduce out-of-pocket and health plan costs for such treatments," the White House press secretary wrote.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signals 25 percent tariffs coming on cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday indicated he would unveil tariffs on imports of automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals in early April, starting at 25 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Zelensky 'should have never started' war with Russia
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday appeared to blame Ukraine's leaders for the three-year war with Russia, arguing Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky "should have never started it.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teacher accused of being intoxicated on school property arrested: Video
item.description: Officials said staff members, who are trained to look for signs of impairments, noticed something about a teacher and contacted the Cheatham County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Andy Cordan, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs executive order asking for recommendations on lowering IVF costs
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order directing federal agencies to find ways to reduce the high cost of in vitro fertilization (IVF) treatment.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trails, bathrooms, wait times: How National Park staffing cuts could impact your next visit
item.description: While thousands of seasonal staff are expected to be hired, the loss of other employees could make your next national park visit feel different.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge refuses to block Musk, DOGE from 7 federal agencies
item.description: U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan on Tuesday refused the request of 14 Democratic state attorneys general to immediately impose wide-ranging restrictions on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: NASA reports increased chance of ‘city killer’ asteroid hitting Earth
item.description: An asteroid big enough to wipe out a city now has an increased chance of hitting Earth in seven years, NASA reported on Tuesday. NASA first discovered the 130-to-300-foot-wide asteroid, named 2024 YR4, in December 2024, finding it only had a roughly 1% chance of impacting Earth on its trajectory.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KTLA 5

item.title: Leonard Peltier leaves prison after Biden commuted his sentence in the killing of two FBI agents
item.description: SUMTERVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Native American activist Leonard Peltier was released from a Florida prison on Tuesday, weeks after then-President Joe Biden angered law enforcement officials by commuting his life sentence to home confinement in the 1975 killings of two FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Ohio State player Ben Christman's cause of death revealed
item.description: The 21-year-old from Akron died on Feb. 11, one day before his 22nd birthday at an off-campus residence near the University of Nevada Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, NewsNation

item.title: NYC voters would likely support Gov. Kathy Hochul removing Adams as mayor, AP VoteCast shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As New York Gov. Kathy Hochul weighs whether to remove New York City Mayor Eric Adams from office, AP VoteCast shows that many New York City voters in last year's election were already on board with the move.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alleged 'Zizians' leader was once declared dead in California
item.description: LaSota was still alive, however, when state police officers found and arrested her Sunday in Allegany County, Maryland.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, NewsNation

item.title: Border Patrol agents told to resume using body cameras
item.description: CBP was one of the first federal agencies to wear body cameras following an order from former President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Former Cleveland kidnapping victims now helping others
item.description: "I felt like this is what I’m supposed to do," Amanda Berry, who hosts a local TV station's 'Missing' segments, said.
item.dc:creator: Cathleen O'Toole, NewsNation

item.title: Leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to 6 killings in multiple states ordered held without bail
item.description: The Zizians have been tied to the killing of U.S. Border Patrol Agent David Maland near the Canadian border in January and five other homicides in Vermont, Pennsylvania and California.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, Fox 5

item.title: Fugitive jailed after melting hospital bed rail to escape
item.description: A man who was wanted for allegedly trying to shoot a gas station employee last year is now behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration tries to bring back fired nuclear weapons workers, reversing DOGE cuts
item.description: he Trump administration has halted the firings of hundreds of federal employees who were tasked with working on the nation's nuclear weapons programs, in an about-face that has left workers confused and experts cautioning that DOGE's blind cost cutting will put communities at risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4
